title: Alocasia puber
intro: Alocasia puber, also known as the hairy elephant ear, is a tropical plant species belonging to the Arum family (Araceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiparasitic properties, which can aid in treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Alocasia puber include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Alocasia puber is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusions, as well as being used in the form of tinctures and ointments, to treat various ailments and promote overall well-being.

Alocasia puber is a relatively rare species, but it is highly valued for its unique and attractive foliage.
